extraction.one_line_summary: POELS Wout won the 18.4km ITT, taking 16 seconds on ALAPHILIPPE Julian and allowing SÁNCHEZ Luis León to retain yellow by 15 seconds over YATES Simon.
tactical_patterns: POELS Wout posted fastest intermediate split then held pressure under wet conditions, suggesting superior technical descending/cornering in rain.
ALAPHILIPPE Julian took maximum risks but lost 16 seconds, indicating Sky's equipment or position optimization was superior to Quick-Step.
SÁNCHEZ Luis León ranked 7th in ITT to defend yellow, showing Astana prioritized GC defense over stage hunting.

rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: Second career ITT win reveals unexpected time trial progression for a pure climber, making him genuine GC threat not just domestique.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Third place despite maximum risk-taking suggests technical ceiling in wet ITT conditions against specialized engines.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: 11th place limiting losses to 5 seconds on SÁNCHEZ shows improved TT discipline compared to historical weakness.
rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: neutral: 7th place defends yellow but conceded time to multiple GC rivals, indicating vulnerability in longer TTs.
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: positive: Held fastest time early before specialists finished, confirming Sky depth in ITT beyond primary leaders.
rider_signals.DE LA CRUZ David: positive: Posted best time before POELS, signaling Sky's systematic advantage in equipment/pacing protocols for ITT stages.
